name: Bean (food medium)
basis: per 100 g raw mature dry seed; Phaseolus vulgaris (common/kidney bean) used as the representative of the generic FooDB 'Bean' pulse record

components.name: water
components.source: USDA FDC 173747 (Beans, kidney, all types, mature seeds, raw; NDB 16033)
components.evidence: Moisture of the raw dry seed.

components.name: starch
components.evidence: DOMINANT carbon/energy source of the dry seed; computed as available carbohydrate minus fiber and free sugars.

components.name: dietary fiber
components.evidence: Largely non-starch polysaccharides + resistant starch; slowly fermentable.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: High-protein pulse; amino-acid pool below is the protein-derived profile.

components.name: sucrose
components.source: USDA FDC 173747 (total sugars 2.23, predominantly sucrose) + Foods 2025 doi:10.3390/foods15020391
components.evidence: Dominant low-molecular-weight free sugar in mature P. vulgaris seed; accounts for essentially all of USDA total sugars.

components.name: stachyose
components.evidence: Dominant raffinose-family oligosaccharide (RFO) in common bean; key fermentable substrate for gut/soil microbes. Not captured in USDA total sugars. Midpoint of reported range.

components.name: raffinose
components.evidence: RFO; fermentable trisaccharide. Midpoint of reported range.

components.name: verbascose
components.evidence: Minor RFO pentasaccharide; fermentable. Midpoint of reported range.

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Trace free monosaccharide in mature dry seed (sucrose >> glucose ~ fructose). Approximate.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Trace free monosaccharide in mature dry seed. Approximate.

components.name: phytic acid (myo-inositol hexakisphosphate)
components.source: P. vulgaris phytate literature; mean ~11.5 mg/g (link.springer 10.1007/BF01091932; Frontiers 2023 doi:10.3389/fsufs.2023.1282427)
components.evidence: Major organic-phosphate storage acid of the seed (~1-1.5% dry weight); chelates Fe/Zn/Ca. Well-quantified.

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: Oxalate content of legumes, J Food Comp Anal doi:10.1016/j.jfca.2004.11.003 (common bean 98-117 mg/100g DW)
components.evidence: Measured whole-seed oxalate range 98-117 mg/100g; midpoint reported.

components.name: malic acid
components.source: NMR metabolomics of P. vulgaris seed, PMC10820859; HPLC of seed extracts (jddtonline 7765)
components.evidence: Confirmed present and one of the two DOMINANT free organic acids of the seed (malic and citric); whole-seed g/100g not standardized across the literature, so amount left null. Typically the leading organic acid, cultivar-dependent.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: NMR metabolomics of P. vulgaris seed, PMC10820859
components.evidence: Confirmed present; co-dominant free organic acid with malic. Whole-seed absolute quantitation not established, amount null.

components.name: lactic acid
components.source: NMR metabolomics of P. vulgaris seed, PMC10820859
components.evidence: Detected at much lower level than citric/malic in raw seed. Amount not quantified.

components.name: L-methionine
components.evidence: Limiting sulfur amino acid in legumes.

components.name: L-aspartate (aspartic acid)
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid (with glutamate); includes asparagine on hydrolysis.

components.name: L-glutamate (glutamic acid)
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid; includes glutamine on hydrolysis.

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: Protein-derived (reported as D-Serine in the FooDB record - stereochemistry error; the biological form is L-serine).

components.name: folate (B9)
components.evidence: Beans are among the richest dietary folate sources.

components.name: vitamin E (alpha-tocopherol)
components.evidence: Alpha-tocopherol; note gamma-tocopherol is the dominant tocopherol in legume seed (see below).

components.name: gamma-tocopherol
components.source: Legume tocopherol literature; USDA reports gamma > alpha in bean seed
components.evidence: Present and typically the predominant tocopherol homolog in P. vulgaris; USDA per-100g value not consistently populated, amount null.

components.name: glycine betaine
components.evidence: Compatible-solute osmolyte present in legume seed; amount not quantified in USDA.

components.name: molybdenum
components.evidence: Legume seeds are a notable dietary Mo source (Mo cofactor); typical reports ~100-350 mcg/100g. Not in USDA panel; amount left null to avoid a fabricated point value. Kept because Mo (molybdate) is a genuine legume micronutrient in the FooDB record.

components.name: alpha-linolenic acid (18:3 n-3)
components.evidence: Bean seed lipid is notably ALA-rich (18:3 >= 18:2).

remove.name: Cholesterol
remove.reason: Animal sterol; not synthesized by plants and absent from bean seed. FooDB predicted/implausible for a plant matrix.

remove.name: Cyanocobalamin
remove.reason: Vitamin B12 is made only by prokaryotes; plants (beans) contain none. USDA lists B12 = 0. FooDB noise.

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: Preformed (animal) vitamin A; beans contain none (USDA vitamin A RAE = 0). Provitamin carotenoids only, at trace level.

remove.name: Lycopene
remove.reason: Tomato/watermelon carotenoid; not a constituent of common bean seed. FooDB noise/misassignment.

remove.name: Beta-Carotene
remove.reason: Negligible/zero in mature dry bean seed (USDA beta-carotene = 0); drop as non-quantifiable trace rather than list a real amount.

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: Cobalt occurs only at ultratrace level and is nutritionally relevant only bound in B12, which beans lack; non-nutritive trace ion.

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Ultratrace, non-nutritive contaminant element, not a defining growth nutrient of bean.

remove.name: Fluoride
remove.reason: Ultratrace, non-nutritive element for a growth medium; not a bean-defining nutrient.

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: Redundant duplicate of iron (already reported once as total iron, 8.2 mg/100g); FooDB lists the same element in multiple redox/compartment forms.

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: Redundant compartment-tagged duplicate of iron; not a separate nutrient.

missing_added: stachyose, raffinose, verbascose (raffinose-family oligosaccharides - major fermentable sugars absent from USDA total-sugars)
phytic acid (major seed organic acid / P store)
malic acid, citric acid, lactic acid (dominant free organic acids, present-but-unquantified)
quantified vitamins (B1/B2/B3/B5/B6, folate, K, E, C, choline)
linoleic acid, total saturated fatty acids, starch, fiber, total protein amounts
keep_uncovered: genistein (isoflavone phytochemical, genuine, no BiGG metabolite)
isoflavones (daidzein/genistein-type; low in common bean but genuine)
gamma-tocopherol (dominant bean tocopherol; keep even if under-parameterized)
kaempferol/quercetin glycosides and proanthocyanidins/anthocyanins (seed-coat polyphenols, genuine)
sources: USDA FDC 173747 (Beans, kidney, all types, mature seeds, raw; NDB 16033) - full macro/mineral/vitamin/amino-acid/fatty-acid panel
Foods 2025 doi:10.3390/foods15020391 (soluble carbohydrates / RFOs in Phaseolus)
Plants 2021 doi:10.3390/plants10071465 (raffinose-family oligosaccharide pathway in P. vulgaris)
J Food Comp Anal doi:10.1016/j.jfca.2004.11.003 (oxalate content of legumes; common bean 98-117 mg/100g)
PMC10820859 (NMR metabolomics of P. vulgaris seed; citric/malic/lactic acids, sucrose, stachyose)
Springer doi:10.1007/BF01091932 & Frontiers 2023 doi:10.3389/fsufs.2023.1282427 (phytic acid ~11.5 mg/g)
ScienceDirect S0889157510000852 (P. vulgaris seed mineral diversity incl. Mo)
notes: Generic FooDB 'Bean' curated as raw mature common/kidney bean (P. vulgaris) seed. DOMINANT carbon/energy source is starch (~33 g/100g); the growth-relevant free-carbohydrate pool is sucrose plus the fermentable raffinose-family oligosaccharides (stachyose > raffinose > verbascose), which USDA total sugars misses. High protein (24 g) gives a rich amino-acid pool (Glu/Asp/Lys-rich). Key organic acids are phytic (~1.15 g), oxalic (~0.1 g), plus malic and citric (present, whole-seed amounts not standardized). Removed animal/implausible FooDB entries (cholesterol, B12, retinol, lycopene) and non-nutritive trace/duplicate ions (Co, Ni, F, duplicate Fe forms).
